- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).

- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).

1	"""
2	Phoenix observability bootstrap for HC-GRC (roadmap Phase 0, deliverable #2).
3	
4	ADR-0009: Phoenix is the local, self-hosted LLM-tracing stack (LangSmith was
5	disqualified as SaaS-only). The Phoenix server runs on the compute node and
6	agent LLM calls export OpenTelemetry traces to it.
7	
8	Two responsibilities, deliberately separated:
9	
10	  1. The SERVER is a long-lived process — run it via the `phoenix serve` CLI
11	     (see launch_server_command()) or the launchd plist in scripts/. It is NOT
12	     launched from inside the application process.
13	
14	  2. INSTRUMENTATION is wired per-process via bootstrap_observability(), called
15	     once by each run entrypoint. It registers a global OTel tracer provider
16	     (BatchSpanProcessor — spans are buffered and exported in the background, so
17	     a momentary server outage drops spans rather than blocking or failing the
18	     run) pointed at the running server, and turns on the LangChain instrumentor.
19	     Because the provider is registered globally, the spans the reasoning_client
20	     emits for BOTH tiers — including Tier-3 (claude-agent-sdk), which LangChain
21	     instrumentation does not see — export to Phoenix too.
22	
23	run_id propagation (ADR-0015 #79): wrap a run in run_trace_context(run_id) so
24	every span produced during the run carries the run_id as session.id + metadata,
25	cross-referencing MLflow / PostgresSaver / PROV-DM.
26	"""
27	
28	from __future__ import annotations
29	
30	import atexit
31	import logging
32	import os
33	import signal
34	import sys
35	import threading
36	from contextlib import contextmanager
37	
38	# ADR-0002: traces carry SCF-derived content and must never leave the machine.
39	# The loopback guard is the single canonical validator in config.py, shared with
40	# Qdrant and MLflow (pass-5) so the data-sovereignty boundary is enforced
41	# identically everywhere; 0.0.0.0 is rejected (it binds all interfaces).
42	from ..config import LOOPBACK_HOSTS as _LOOPBACK_HOSTS  # noqa: F401  (kept for callers/tests)
43	from ..config import load_platform_config
44	from ..config import require_loopback as _require_loopback
45	
46	_log = logging.getLogger(__name__)
47	
48	# Cached registered provider — registration is idempotent within a process
49	# (repeat bootstrap calls return the same provider instead of double-registering
50	# the instrumentor, which would duplicate every span).
51	_PROVIDER = None
52	
53	
54	def _phoenix_cfg() -> dict:
55	    return load_platform_config()["observability"]["phoenix"]
56	
57	
58	def phoenix_base_url() -> str:
59	    """Return the Phoenix server base URL, e.g. http://localhost:6006."""
60	    cfg = _phoenix_cfg()
61	    return f"http://{cfg.get('host', 'localhost')}:{cfg.get('port', 6006)}"
62	
63	
64	def phoenix_endpoint() -> str:
65	    """Return the OTLP/HTTP traces endpoint the tracer provider exports to.
66	
67	    Validates loopback at the source (pass-5 #obs-wiring): this is public API and
68	    is called from the __main__ entrypoint and tests, not only from the already-
69	    guarded instrument_langchain(), so it must enforce its own ADR-0002 precondition."""
70	    cfg = _phoenix_cfg()
71	    _require_loopback(str(cfg.get("host", "localhost")), "Phoenix OTLP export")
72	    return f"{phoenix_base_url()}/v1/traces"
73	
74	
75	def launch_server_command() -> str:
76	    """Return the shell command that starts the persistent Phoenix server.
77	
78	    Validates the bind host is loopback (#283): the server bind, not just the
79	    client export host, is what determines whether traces are reachable off-box.
80	    """
81	    cfg = _phoenix_cfg()
82	    host = _require_loopback(str(cfg.get("host", "localhost")), "server bind")
83	    return f"phoenix serve --host {host} --port {cfg.get('port', 6006)}"
84	
85	
86	def instrument_langchain(project_name: str | None = None):
87	    """
88	    Register a global OTel tracer provider pointed at the Phoenix server (using a
89	    background BatchSpanProcessor) and enable LangChain tracing for this process.
90	
91	    Idempotent: the first call registers; subsequent calls return the cached
92	    provider without re-instrumenting (so spans are never duplicated).
93	
94	    Returns the tracer provider.
95	    """
96	    global _PROVIDER
97	    if _PROVIDER is not None:
98	        return _PROVIDER
99	
100	    from openinference.instrumentation.langchain import LangChainInstrumentor
101	    from opentelemetry import trace as _trace
102	    from phoenix.otel import register
103	
104	    cfg = _phoenix_cfg()
105	    # ADR-0002 data sovereignty: refuse to export SCF-derived traces off-box.
106	    _require_loopback(str(cfg.get("host", "localhost")), "OTLP export")
107	
108	    # Bound the OTLP/HTTP exporter so a downed Phoenix server cannot block process
109	    # teardown for the exporter's default 10s on the shutdown flush. The installed
110	    # OTel BatchProcessor.force_flush() ignores its timeout_millis argument, so the
111	    # only effective cap is at the exporter level (#277). Don't clobber an explicit
112	    # operator override.
113	    os.environ.setdefault("OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_TRACES_TIMEOUT", "2")
114	
115	    provider = register(
116	        project_name=project_name or cfg.get("project_name", "hc-grc"),
117	        endpoint=phoenix_endpoint(),
118	        batch=True,                        # BatchSpanProcessor: buffer + async export
119	        set_global_tracer_provider=True,   # so reasoning_client spans export too
120	    )
121	    # set_tracer_provider can only succeed once per process; if a competing global
122	    # provider already won the race, register() still RETURNS the Phoenix provider
123	    # (so LangChain spans, passed it explicitly, still reach Phoenix) but the GLOBAL
124	    # provider is something else — and reasoning_client's module-level tracer resolves
125	    # through the global, so Tier-3 frontier spans (their ONLY record) would silently
126	    # vanish. Surface that collision instead of dropping them silently (#278).
127	    if _trace.get_tracer_provider() is not provider:
128	        _log.warning(
129	            "Phoenix did not become the global OTel tracer provider — a competing "
130	            "provider was registered first. Tier-3 reasoning_client spans will not "
131	            "reach Phoenix. Ensure bootstrap_observability() runs before any other "
132	            "OTel instrumentation."
133	        )
134	    LangChainInstrumentor().instrument(tracer_provider=provider, skip_dep_check=True)
135	    # Flush buffered spans at process exit so the BatchSpanProcessor does not drop
136	    # the tail of a run on a clean exit / SIGINT (#215). atexit does NOT run on
137	    # SIGTERM (launchd/kill/container-stop), so also install a SIGTERM handler that
138	    # flushes then exits — otherwise the tail of a killed run is silently lost
139	    # (#281). Both flushes are bounded by the exporter timeout set above (#277).
140	    if hasattr(provider, "force_flush"):
141	        atexit.register(provider.force_flush)
142	        _install_sigterm_flush(provider)
143	    _PROVIDER = provider
144	    return provider
145	
146	
147	def _install_sigterm_flush(provider) -> None:
148	    """Flush + shut down the span processor on SIGTERM before the process exits.
149	
150	    Signal handlers can only be installed from the main thread; if we're not on it
151	    (e.g. instrumentation wired from a worker), skip silently — atexit still covers
152	    the clean-exit and SIGINT paths."""
153	    if threading.current_thread() is not threading.main_thread():
154	        return
155	
156	    def _handler(_signum, _frame):
157	        try:
158	            provider.force_flush()
159	            if hasattr(provider, "shutdown"):
160	                provider.shutdown()
161	        finally:
162	            sys.exit(0)
163	
164	    try:
165	        signal.signal(signal.SIGTERM, _handler)
166	    except (ValueError, OSError):  # pragma: no cover - non-main-thread / unsupported
167	        pass
168	
169	
170	def bootstrap_observability():
171	    """
172	    Idempotently wire tracing for the current process. Called once by each run
173	    entrypoint (graph runners). Degrades to a no-op — never raising — when tracing
174	    is disabled by config, switched off via HCGRC_DISABLE_TRACING, or the
175	    instrumentation stack cannot be initialized. Returns the provider or None.
176	    """
177	    if os.environ.get("HCGRC_DISABLE_TRACING", "").strip():
178	        return None
179	    try:
180	        cfg = _phoenix_cfg()
181	    except Exception:
182	        return None
183	    if not cfg.get("enabled", True):
184	        return None
185	    # Also point MLflow at the local store so experiment tracking is actually
186	    # configured in a run path (cross-store run_id correlation, #282). Best-effort.
187	    try:
188	        from ..tracking.mlflow_setup import configure_mlflow
189	        configure_mlflow()
190	    except Exception:
191	        pass
192	    try:
193	        return instrument_langchain()
194	    except Exception:
195	        # Missing deps, server unreachable at registration, etc. — tracing is
196	        # best-effort observability and must never block the research run.
197	        return None
198	
199	
200	@contextmanager
201	def run_trace_context(run_id: str | None):
202	    """
203	    Context manager that stamps run_id onto every span produced within it, for
204	    cross-store correlation. Sets it two ways so BOTH span sources pick it up:
205	    OpenInference using_attributes (LangChain auto-spans → session.id) AND OTel
206	    baggage `hcgrc.run_id`, which the reasoning_client seam reads as a fallback so
207	    its manual T2/T3 spans carry the run_id even when the caller didn't pass it
208	    explicitly (#239). No-op if run_id is None or the helpers are unavailable.
209	    """
210	    if not run_id:
211	        yield
212	        return
213	    from contextlib import ExitStack
214	
215	    from opentelemetry import baggage, context
216	
217	    with ExitStack() as stack:
218	        try:
219	            from openinference.instrumentation import using_attributes
220	            stack.enter_context(using_attributes(session_id=run_id, metadata={"run_id": run_id}))
221	        except Exception:
222	            pass
223	        token = context.attach(baggage.set_baggage("hcgrc.run_id", run_id))
224	        try:
225	            yield
226	        finally:
227	            context.detach(token)
228	
229	
230	if __name__ == "__main__":  # pragma: no cover - operational entrypoint
231	    print(f"Phoenix base URL:    {phoenix_base_url()}")
232	    print(f"OTLP traces endpoint: {phoenix_endpoint()}")
233	    print(f"Start the server with: {launch_server_command()}")

1	"""
2	Platform configuration loader.
3	
4	Single source of truth for reading configs/platform.yaml. Infrastructure
5	modules use this so host/port/uri parameters are never hardcoded — they
6	track the version-controlled config.
7	"""
8	
9	from __future__ import annotations
10	
11	import functools
12	from pathlib import Path
13	from typing import Any
14	from urllib.parse import urlparse
15	
16	import yaml
17	
18	
19	def repo_root() -> Path:
20	    """Return the repository root (three levels up from this file)."""
21	    return Path(__file__).resolve().parents[2]
22	
23	
24	class ConfigError(ValueError):
25	    """Raised when configs/platform.yaml is malformed or missing required keys."""
26	
27	
28	# ── ADR-0002 data-sovereignty: the canonical loopback guard ───────────────────
29	# SCF-derived content (embeddings, traces, experiment metadata) must NEVER leave
30	# the compute node. This is the single shared validator used by EVERY infra module
31	# that takes a host/URI from config — Phoenix, Qdrant, MLflow — so the boundary is
32	# enforced identically everywhere instead of one guard per module (pass-5).
33	LOOPBACK_HOSTS = {"localhost", "127.0.0.1", "::1"}
34	
35	
36	def require_loopback(host: str, what: str) -> str:
37	    """Return host if it is loopback, else raise ConfigError. 0.0.0.0 is rejected:
38	    it binds all interfaces, exposing data off-box — exactly what ADR-0002 forbids."""
39	    if host not in LOOPBACK_HOSTS:
40	        raise ConfigError(
41	            f"{what} host {host!r} is not loopback. SCF-derived content must stay on "
42	            f"the compute node (ADR-0002). Use one of {sorted(LOOPBACK_HOSTS)}."
43	        )
44	    return host
45	
46	
47	def require_local_uri(uri: str, what: str) -> str:
48	    """Validate a backing-store URI is local (ADR-0002). A bare path or sqlite:///
49	    file is local; any networked scheme (http/https/postgresql/mysql/mssql) must
50	    point at a loopback host. Rejects remote tracking servers that would exfiltrate."""
51	    if uri.startswith("sqlite:") or "://" not in uri:
52	        return uri  # local sqlite file or a bare relative directory store
53	    host = (urlparse(uri).hostname or "").strip()
54	    # No host component (e.g. file:///path) is local; a host must be loopback.
55	    if host and host not in LOOPBACK_HOSTS:
56	        raise ConfigError(
57	            f"{what} URI host {host!r} is not loopback ({uri!r}). Experiment/tracking "
58	            f"data must stay on the compute node (ADR-0002)."
59	        )
60	    return uri
61	
62	
63	# Required structure (dotted paths). Validated at load so a silent typo or a
64	# dropped section fails loudly at startup rather than as a confusing KeyError deep
65	# in an infrastructure module (#171).
66	_REQUIRED_PATHS = (
67	    "observability.phoenix.port",
68	    "observability.phoenix.project_name",
69	    "observability.mlflow.tracking_uri",
70	    "vector_store.host",       # dereferenced by qdrant_setup (#207)
71	    "vector_store.http_port",
72	    "checkpointing.backend",   # drives PostgresSaver selection (#271)
73	    "checkpointing.database",

77	def _validate_config(cfg: Any, source: str) -> dict[str, Any]:
78	    if not isinstance(cfg, dict):
79	        raise ConfigError(f"{source}: top-level YAML must be a mapping, got {type(cfg).__name__}.")
80	    for path in _REQUIRED_PATHS:
81	        node: Any = cfg
82	        for key in path.split("."):
83	            if not isinstance(node, dict) or key not in node:
84	                raise ConfigError(f"{source}: missing required config key '{path}'.")
85	            node = node[key]
86	        # A key present but null (YAML `key:` with no value) is as broken as a
87	        # missing one — it sails through the presence check then blows up downstream
88	        # as a TypeError. Reject it here (pass-7).
89	        if node is None:
90	            raise ConfigError(f"{source}: required config key '{path}' is null.")
91	
92	    # checkpointing.backend selects the checkpointer; an unrecognized value silently
93	    # falls through to the Postgres path. Validate the enum at load (pass-7).
94	    backend = cfg["checkpointing"]["backend"]
95	    if backend not in ("memory", "postgres"):
96	        raise ConfigError(

98	        )
99	
100	    # ADR-0002 fail-fast: enforce loopback/local on every host/URI value at LOAD
101	    # time, not just at point-of-use, so a misconfigured platform.yaml is rejected
102	    # at startup rather than silently exfiltrating to a remote host (pass-5). These
103	    # are value checks; the loop above only checked key presence.
104	    require_loopback(str(cfg["vector_store"]["host"]), f"{source}: vector_store")
105	    require_local_uri(

107	    )
108	    phoenix = cfg["observability"].get("phoenix", {})
109	    if isinstance(phoenix, dict) and "host" in phoenix:
110	        require_loopback(str(phoenix["host"]), f"{source}: observability.phoenix")
111	    checkpointing = cfg.get("checkpointing", {})
112	    if isinstance(checkpointing, dict) and "host" in checkpointing:
113	        require_loopback(str(checkpointing["host"]), f"{source}: checkpointing")

122	    Cached for the process lifetime — platform-scope config is stable within a
123	    run. Call load_platform_config.cache_clear() in tests that mutate the file.

=== instrument_langchain ===
def instrument_langchain(project_name: str | None = None):
    """
    Register a global OTel tracer provider pointed at the Phoenix server (using a
    background BatchSpanProcessor) and enable LangChain tracing for this process.

    Idempotent: the first call registers; subsequent calls return the cached
    provider without re-instrumenting (so spans are never duplicated).

    Returns the tracer provider.
    """
    global _PROVIDER
    if _PROVIDER is not None:
        return _PROVIDER

    from openinference.instrumentation.langchain import LangChainInstrumentor
    from opentelemetry import trace as _trace
    from phoenix.otel import register

    cfg = _phoenix_cfg()
    # ADR-0002 data sovereignty: refuse to export SCF-derived traces off-box.
    _require_loopback(str(cfg.get("host", "localhost")), "OTLP export")

    # Bound the OTLP/HTTP exporter so a downed Phoenix server cannot block process
    # teardown for the exporter's default 10s on the shutdown flush. The installed
    # OTel BatchProcessor.force_flush() ignores its timeout_millis argument, so the
    # only effective cap is at the exporter level (#277). Don't clobber an explicit
    # operator override.
    os.environ.setdefault("OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_TRACES_TIMEOUT", "2")

    provider = register(
        project_name=project_name or cfg.get("project_name", "hc-grc"),
        endpoint=phoenix_endpoint(),
        batch=True,                        # BatchSpanProcessor: buffer + async export
        set_global_tracer_provider=True,   # so reasoning_client spans export too
    )
    # set_tracer_provider can only succeed once per process; if a competing global
    # provider already won the race, register() still RETURNS the Phoenix provider
    # (so LangChain spans, passed it explicitly, still reach Phoenix) but the GLOBAL
    # provider is something else — and reasoning_client's module-level tracer resolves
    # through the global, so Tier-3 frontier spans (their ONLY record) would silently
    # vanish. Surface that collision instead of dropping them silently (#278).
    if _trace.get_tracer_provider() is not provider:
        _log.warning(
            "Phoenix did not become the global OTel tracer provider — a competing "
            "provider was registered first. Tier-3 reasoning_client spans will not "
            "reach Phoenix. Ensure bootstrap_observability() runs before any other "
            "OTel instrumentation."
        )
    LangChainInstrumentor().instrument(tracer_provider=provider, skip_dep_check=True)
    # Flush buffered spans at process exit so the BatchSpanProcessor does not drop
    # the tail of a run on a clean exit / SIGINT (#215). atexit does NOT run on
    # SIGTERM (launchd/kill/container-stop), so also install a SIGTERM handler that
    # flushes then exits — otherwise the tail of a killed run is silently lost
    # (#281). Both flushes are bounded by the exporter timeout set above (#277).
    if hasattr(provider, "force_flush"):
        atexit.register(provider.force_flush)
        _install_sigterm_flush(provider)
    _PROVIDER = provider
    return provider

    """
    Build and compile the HC-GRC LangGraph graph.

    builder.add_node("orchestrator", t00_orchestrator_node)

    # Phase 0 wrapper: synthetic IDs matching SCF scale for gate dry-runs.
    def _data_split_phase0(state: HCGRCState) -> dict[str, Any]:

1	"""
2	Phoenix observability bootstrap for HC-GRC (roadmap Phase 0, deliverable #2).
3	
4	ADR-0009: Phoenix is the local, self-hosted LLM-tracing stack (LangSmith was
5	disqualified as SaaS-only). The Phoenix server runs on the compute node and
6	agent LLM calls export OpenTelemetry traces to it.
7	
8	Two responsibilities, deliberately separated:
9	
10	  1. The SERVER is a long-lived process — run it via the `phoenix serve` CLI
11	     (see launch_server_command()) or the launchd plist in scripts/. It is NOT
12	     launched from inside the application process.
13	
14	  2. INSTRUMENTATION is wired per-process via bootstrap_observability(), called
15	     once by each run entrypoint. It registers a global OTel tracer provider
16	     (BatchSpanProcessor — spans are buffered and exported in the background, so
17	     a momentary server outage drops spans rather than blocking or failing the
18	     run) pointed at the running server, and turns on the LangChain instrumentor.
19	     Because the provider is registered globally, the spans the reasoning_client
20	     emits for BOTH tiers — including Tier-3 (claude-agent-sdk), which LangChain
21	     instrumentation does not see — export to Phoenix too.
22	
23	run_id propagation (ADR-0015 #79): wrap a run in run_trace_context(run_id) so
24	every span produced during the run carries the run_id as session.id + metadata,
25	cross-referencing MLflow / PostgresSaver / PROV-DM.
26	"""
27	
28	from __future__ import annotations
29	
30	import atexit
31	import logging
32	import os
33	import signal
34	import sys
35	import threading
36	from contextlib import contextmanager
37	
38	# ADR-0002: traces carry SCF-derived content and must never leave the machine.
39	# The loopback guard is the single canonical validator in config.py, shared with
40	# Qdrant and MLflow (pass-5) so the data-sovereignty boundary is enforced
41	# identically everywhere; 0.0.0.0 is rejected (it binds all interfaces).
42	from ..config import LOOPBACK_HOSTS as _LOOPBACK_HOSTS  # noqa: F401  (kept for callers/tests)
43	from ..config import load_platform_config
44	from ..config import require_loopback as _require_loopback
45	
46	_log = logging.getLogger(__name__)
47	
48	# Cached registered provider — registration is idempotent within a process
49	# (repeat bootstrap calls return the same provider instead of double-registering
50	# the instrumentor, which would duplicate every span).
51	_PROVIDER = None
52	
53	
54	def _phoenix_cfg() -> dict:
55	    return load_platform_config()["observability"]["phoenix"]
56	
57	
58	def phoenix_base_url() -> str:
59	    """Return the Phoenix server base URL, e.g. http://localhost:6006."""
60	    cfg = _phoenix_cfg()
61	    return f"http://{cfg.get('host', 'localhost')}:{cfg.get('port', 6006)}"
62	
63	
64	def phoenix_endpoint() -> str:
65	    """Return the OTLP/HTTP traces endpoint the tracer provider exports to.
66	
67	    Validates loopback at the source (pass-5 #obs-wiring): this is public API and
68	    is called from the __main__ entrypoint and tests, not only from the already-
69	    guarded instrument_langchain(), so it must enforce its own ADR-0002 precondition."""
70	    cfg = _phoenix_cfg()
71	    _require_loopback(str(cfg.get("host", "localhost")), "Phoenix OTLP export")
72	    return f"{phoenix_base_url()}/v1/traces"
73	
74	
75	def launch_server_command() -> str:
76	    """Return the shell command that starts the persistent Phoenix server.
77	
78	    Validates the bind host is loopback (#283): the server bind, not just the
79	    client export host, is what determines whether traces are reachable off-box.
80	    """
81	    cfg = _phoenix_cfg()
82	    host = _require_loopback(str(cfg.get("host", "localhost")), "server bind")
83	    return f"phoenix serve --host {host} --port {cfg.get('port', 6006)}"
84	
85	
86	def instrument_langchain(project_name: str | None = None):
87	    """
88	    Register a global OTel tracer provider pointed at the Phoenix server (using a
89	    background BatchSpanProcessor) and enable LangChain tracing for this process.
90	
91	    Idempotent: the first call registers; subsequent calls return the cached
92	    provider without re-instrumenting (so spans are never duplicated).
93	
94	    Returns the tracer provider.
95	    """
96	    global _PROVIDER
97	    if _PROVIDER is not None:
98	        return _PROVIDER
99	
100	    from openinference.instrumentation.langchain import LangChainInstrumentor
101	    from opentelemetry import trace as _trace
102	    from phoenix.otel import register
103	
104	    cfg = _phoenix_cfg()
105	    # ADR-0002 data sovereignty: refuse to export SCF-derived traces off-box.
106	    _require_loopback(str(cfg.get("host", "localhost")), "OTLP export")
107	
108	    # Bound the OTLP/HTTP exporter so a downed Phoenix server cannot block process
109	    # teardown for the exporter's default 10s on the shutdown flush. The installed
110	    # OTel BatchProcessor.force_flush() ignores its timeout_millis argument, so the
111	    # only effective cap is at the exporter level (#277). Don't clobber an explicit
112	    # operator override.
113	    os.environ.setdefault("OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_TRACES_TIMEOUT", "2")
114	
115	    provider = register(
116	        project_name=project_name or cfg.get("project_name", "hc-grc"),
117	        endpoint=phoenix_endpoint(),
118	        batch=True,                        # BatchSpanProcessor: buffer + async export
119	        set_global_tracer_provider=True,   # so reasoning_client spans export too
120	    )
121	    # set_tracer_provider can only succeed once per process; if a competing global
122	    # provider already won the race, register() still RETURNS the Phoenix provider
123	    # (so LangChain spans, passed it explicitly, still reach Phoenix) but the GLOBAL
124	    # provider is something else — and reasoning_client's module-level tracer resolves
125	    # through the global, so Tier-3 frontier spans (their ONLY record) would silently
126	    # vanish. Surface that collision instead of dropping them silently (#278).
127	    if _trace.get_tracer_provider() is not provider:
128	        _log.warning(
129	            "Phoenix did not become the global OTel tracer provider — a competing "
130	            "provider was registered first. Tier-3 reasoning_client spans will not "
131	            "reach Phoenix. Ensure bootstrap_observability() runs before any other "
132	            "OTel instrumentation."
133	        )
134	    LangChainInstrumentor().instrument(tracer_provider=provider, skip_dep_check=True)
135	    # Flush buffered spans at process exit so the BatchSpanProcessor does not drop
136	    # the tail of a run on a clean exit / SIGINT (#215). atexit does NOT run on
137	    # SIGTERM (launchd/kill/container-stop), so also install a SIGTERM handler that
138	    # flushes then exits — otherwise the tail of a killed run is silently lost
139	    # (#281). Both flushes are bounded by the exporter timeout set above (#277).
140	    if hasattr(provider, "force_flush"):
141	        atexit.register(provider.force_flush)
142	        _install_sigterm_flush(provider)
143	    _PROVIDER = provider
144	    return provider
145	
146	
147	def _install_sigterm_flush(provider) -> None:
148	    """Flush + shut down the span processor on SIGTERM before the process exits.
149	
150	    Signal handlers can only be installed from the main thread; if we're not on it
151	    (e.g. instrumentation wired from a worker), skip silently — atexit still covers
152	    the clean-exit and SIGINT paths."""
153	    if threading.current_thread() is not threading.main_thread():
154	        return
155	
156	    def _handler(_signum, _frame):
157	        try:
158	            provider.force_flush()
159	            if hasattr(provider, "shutdown"):
160	                provider.shutdown()
161	        finally:
162	            sys.exit(0)
163	
164	    try:
165	        signal.signal(signal.SIGTERM, _handler)
166	    except (ValueError, OSError):  # pragma: no cover - non-main-thread / unsupported
167	        pass
168	
169	
170	def bootstrap_observability():
171	    """
172	    Idempotently wire tracing for the current process. Called once by each run
173	    entrypoint (graph runners). Degrades to a no-op — never raising — when tracing
174	    is disabled by config, switched off via HCGRC_DISABLE_TRACING, or the
175	    instrumentation stack cannot be initialized. Returns the provider or None.
176	    """
177	    if os.environ.get("HCGRC_DISABLE_TRACING", "").strip():
178	        return None
179	    try:
180	        cfg = _phoenix_cfg()
181	    except Exception:
182	        return None
183	    if not cfg.get("enabled", True):
184	        return None
185	    # Also point MLflow at the local store so experiment tracking is actually
186	    # configured in a run path (cross-store run_id correlation, #282). Best-effort.
187	    try:
188	        from ..tracking.mlflow_setup import configure_mlflow
189	        configure_mlflow()
190	    except Exception:
191	        pass
192	    try:
193	        return instrument_langchain()
194	    except Exception:
195	        # Missing deps, server unreachable at registration, etc. — tracing is
196	        # best-effort observability and must never block the research run.
197	        return None
198	
199	
200	@contextmanager
201	def run_trace_context(run_id: str | None):
202	    """
203	    Context manager that stamps run_id onto every span produced within it, for
204	    cross-store correlation. Sets it two ways so BOTH span sources pick it up:
205	    OpenInference using_attributes (LangChain auto-spans → session.id) AND OTel
206	    baggage `hcgrc.run_id`, which the reasoning_client seam reads as a fallback so
207	    its manual T2/T3 spans carry the run_id even when the caller didn't pass it
208	    explicitly (#239). No-op if run_id is None or the helpers are unavailable.
209	    """
210	    if not run_id:
211	        yield
212	        return
213	    from contextlib import ExitStack
214	
215	    from opentelemetry import baggage, context
216	
217	    with ExitStack() as stack:
218	        try:
219	            from openinference.instrumentation import using_attributes
220	            stack.enter_context(using_attributes(session_id=run_id, metadata={"run_id": run_id}))
221	        except Exception:
222	            pass
223	        token = context.attach(baggage.set_baggage("hcgrc.run_id", run_id))
224	        try:
225	            yield
226	        finally:
227	            context.detach(token)
228	
229	
230	if __name__ == "__main__":  # pragma: no cover - operational entrypoint
231	    print(f"Phoenix base URL:    {phoenix_base_url()}")
232	    print(f"OTLP traces endpoint: {phoenix_endpoint()}")
233	    print(f"Start the server with: {launch_server_command()}")
